refnamediv.refpurpose: watch macro library source directories and hot-reload them on change

listitem.para: a string, the name of a loaded library (as returned by librarieslist).
a string, the path of the directory holding the library's *.sci source files.

refsection.title: Description
refsection.para: macroswatch enables live hot-reload of a macro library: it watches the library's source directory and, when one of its *.sci files is changed by another program (a build, a version-control checkout, or a command run in the embedded terminal), it automatically re-runs genlib so the updated function becomes live in the interpreter - not merely changed on disk.
The reload is queued and runs at the interpreter prompt: workspace variables are untouched and a function currently executing finishes on its old code, while subsequent calls use the new code.
macroswatch() with no argument watches every loaded user library (Scilab's own libraries, under SCI, are left alone). macroswatch(library_name, directory) watches a single library.
File watching uses the native change-notification service, so it is immediate and recursive. This function requires the Scilab graphical interface.

refsection.title: Examples
